title: Implement crash recovery for channels, cursors, and conversations
purpose: Implements crash recovery for all durable components: channels reconstruct partition sequence numbers, conversations replay their event logs and resume from the last completed step, and consumer cursors read their persisted offsets and replay from the checkpoint. Recovery is replay, not reconstruction (P4) — the normal read path is the recovery path. This brief orchestrates the recovery sequence that brings a crashed bus instance back to consistent state without manual intervention.
task: Build the crash recovery module that coordinates recovery across durable channels, conversations, and consumer cursors. Channel recovery reads the haematite log for each partition to determine the current sequence number. Conversation recovery replays the event log (using DUR-005's replay logic) and resumes from the last completed step. Cursor recovery reads the persisted offset (using DUR-003's resume logic) and replays messages from that point. The recovery module must guarantee idempotent replay: replaying the same event log twice produces identical state. Scope boundary: this brief orchestrates recovery. The individual replay and resume operations are implemented in DUR-003 and DUR-005 — this brief calls them, not reimplements them.

requirements.title: Recover durable channel partition sequences from haematite
requirements.spec: WHEN a durable channel recovers after crash, THE SYSTEM SHALL read each partition's log from haematite using DurableStore::read_from to determine the current sequence number. The recovered sequence number SHALL be set to the highest sequence number found in the partition log, or 0 if the log is empty. This reconstructed sequence SHALL be used as expected_seq for subsequent appends.
acceptance: A channel with 3 partitions recovers each partition's sequence independently.
A partition with stored events 0..41 recovers with sequence 42 (next expected).
An empty partition recovers with sequence 0.
The recovered sequence matches what DUR-002's append path expects.
Recovery reads from haematite, not from any in-memory cache.

files: crates/liminal/src/durability/store.rs:16-39 — `DurableStore` trait; recovery must use `read_from` and must not call `append`/`cas`.
crates/liminal/src/durability/channel/storage.rs:36-44 — `DurableChannel` fields include per-partition `next_sequences` and the store handle.
crates/liminal/src/durability/channel/storage.rs:83-102 — `DurableChannel::from_recovered_sequences` is the constructor recovery should feed.
crates/liminal/src/durability/channel/storage.rs:152-178 — stream key format and append path: `"{channel_id}:{partition_index}"`, `sequence_for_append`, then `append(... expected_seq)`.
crates/liminal/src/durability/mod.rs:1-15 — mod file is declarations/re-exports only; add `pub mod recovery;` and any `pub use` without logic.
context: Channel sequence counters represent the next expected sequence, not the last stored sequence. The brief wording says highest found or 0, but acceptance clarifies stored events `0..41` recover as `42`; implement `last_sequence + 1`, with empty log as `0`.

`DurableChannel::from_recovered_sequences` already validates `partition_count != 0` and that the sequence vector length equals the partition count, so prefer returning that type rather than inventing parallel state.
The append path expects independent counters per partition and uses `stream_key_for(partition_index)` format `channel_id:partition_index`; recovery must generate the same keys for all partitions.
There is no in-memory cache path in channel storage today; recovery should read each partition from the store directly and deterministically.
scout.approach: Create `crates/liminal/src/durability/recovery.rs` with a small `READ_BATCH_SIZE` constant and a public async helper such as `recover_durable_channel(channel_id, partition_count, store) -> Result<DurableChannel, DurabilityError>` or `recover_partition_sequences(...) -> Result<Vec<u64>, DurabilityError>` plus a constructor wrapper. For each partition index `0..partition_count`, build the same stream key used by `DurableChannel` (`format!("{channel_id}:{partition_index}")`), page with `store.read_from(&key, offset, READ_BATCH_SIZE)`, track the last `StoredEntry.sequence`, and compute `next_seq = last_sequence.checked_add(1)?` or `0` if no entries. Pass the recovered vector into `DurableChannel::from_recovered_sequences` so subsequent publishes use those values as `expected_seq`.
scout.notes: Avoid assuming `offset == sequence`; existing replay code advances the read offset by batch length while separately tracking `stored.sequence`. Also guard `checked_add` on both read offsets and recovered sequence; use `DurabilityError::ConfigError` for overflow because no recovery-specific error types are allowed.

files_changed.note: Added read-only channel recovery helpers that page each partition stream with DurableStore::read_from and compute next expected sequence as last stored sequence + 1, or 0 for empty partitions.
Added tests for independent partition recovery, empty partitions, idempotent sequence reconstruction, read_from usage, and subsequent append expected_seq compatibility.
Declared and re-exported the recovery module helpers while keeping mod.rs to declarations/re-exports only.

dev.how: `recover_partition_sequences` builds the same `channel_id:partition_index` keys used by `DurableChannel`, reads haematite directly via `read_from` in pages, tracks stored entry sequences independently per partition, and feeds the recovered vector into `DurableChannel::from_recovered_sequences`. Overflow is reported as `DurabilityError::ConfigError`; no append/cas path is used by recovery.

checklist.note: Crash recovery reconstructs durable channel partition sequence numbers from persisted partition logs and tests cover 3 independent partitions including an empty one.

stories.note: Durable channel state can be reconstructed automatically from haematite on restart with no manual sequence repair.

acceptance.criterion: A channel with 3 partitions recovers each partition's sequence independently.
acceptance.evidence: crates/liminal/src/durability/recovery.rs:30-40 iterates each partition index and recovers a separate stream key; test durability::recovery::tests::channel_recovery_reconstructs_partition_sequences asserts [42, 0, 5] at crates/liminal/src/durability/recovery/tests.rs:17-21.

acceptance.criterion: A partition with stored events 0..41 recovers with sequence 42 (next expected).
acceptance.evidence: crates/liminal/src/durability/recovery.rs:115-131 tracks the last stored sequence and returns last + 1; the populated test seeds 42 events and asserts next sequence 42 at crates/liminal/src/durability/recovery/tests.rs:14,20.

acceptance.criterion: An empty partition recovers with sequence 0.
acceptance.evidence: crates/liminal/src/durability/recovery.rs:112-125 returns 0 when no last_sequence exists; the 3-partition test leaves orders:1 empty and asserts the middle sequence is 0 at crates/liminal/src/durability/recovery/tests.rs:20-21.

acceptance.criterion: The recovered sequence matches what DUR-002's append path expects.
acceptance.evidence: crates/liminal/src/durability/recovery.rs:54-56 feeds recovered sequences into DurableChannel::from_recovered_sequences; DurableChannel::publish uses sequence_for_append as expected_seq at crates/liminal/src/durability/channel/storage.rs:165-173. The test publishes after recovery and records expected_seq 42 at crates/liminal/src/durability/recovery/tests.rs:40-45.

acceptance.criterion: Recovery reads from haematite, not from any in-memory cache.
acceptance.evidence: crates/liminal/src/durability/recovery.rs:103-124 uses DurableStore::read_from in the recovery loop; the test records read_from calls for orders:0, orders:1, and orders:2 at crates/liminal/src/durability/recovery/tests.rs:24-38.

requirements.title: Recover durable conversations via event log replay
requirements.spec: WHEN a durable conversation recovers after crash, THE SYSTEM SHALL replay its event log from haematite using DUR-005's replay logic. The replayed ConversationState SHALL determine: which messages are fully processed (skip on re-delivery), which are partially processed (resume from last step), and what the current expected_seq is for new appends. THE SYSTEM SHALL NOT use any state cache — replay from the event log is the sole recovery path.
acceptance: A conversation with events [MessageReceived, ProcessingStarted, StepCompleted(0)] recovers with step 0 complete and processing resumable from step 1.
A conversation with ProcessingFinished for a message recovers with that message marked as fully processed.
Recovery delegates to DUR-005's replay logic, not a reimplementation.
The recovered conversation's expected_seq is set to last_event_sequence + 1.
No in-memory cache is consulted — only haematite.

files: crates/liminal/src/durability/conversation.rs:67-145 — `ConversationState` is `PartialEq` and replay/apply is idempotent set/map mutation.
crates/liminal/src/durability/conversation.rs:158-196 — `DurableConversation::recover` already replays from haematite and sets `expected_seq`.
crates/liminal/src/durability/conversation.rs:216-239 — redelivery behavior uses replayed state to `Skip`, `ResumeFrom`, or `Start`.
crates/liminal/src/durability/conversation.rs:357-391 — private `replay_stream` pages `read_from`, deserializes events, applies state, and computes `last_sequence + 1`.
crates/liminal/src/durability/conversation/tests.rs:86-116 — existing test shows recovery replays persisted events, sets expected sequence, and records read offsets.
context: DUR-005 replay logic is already implemented as `DurableConversation::recover`; because `replay_stream` is private, recovery orchestration should delegate to `DurableConversation::recover` rather than duplicating event decoding.
`ConversationState` derives `PartialEq, Eq`, so idempotency tests can compare full states directly.
The replay result drives fully processed vs partial handling: `finished_messages` makes `receive_message` return `Skip`; `received_messages` plus completed steps produces `ResumeFrom(next_step)`.
Append code in conversations uses `self.expected_seq` and then updates state after successful append; recovered expected seq must remain the value from replay.
scout.approach: In `recovery.rs`, expose a thin helper such as `recover_conversation(conversation_id, store) -> Result<DurableConversation, DurabilityError>` that simply calls `DurableConversation::recover(conversation_id, store).await`. Tests for DUR-006 should seed the store with conversation events via normal `DurableConversation::record_*` calls or serialized events, then call the recovery module and assert `state.last_completed_step("msg") == Some(0)`, `receive_message("msg", ...)` returns `ResumeFrom(1)` for partial processing, `state.is_fully_processed("msg")` for finished processing, and `expected_seq() == last_event_sequence + 1`.
scout.notes: Do not move or copy `replay_stream` into `recovery.rs` unless you intentionally make it visible for reuse; copying it would violate the boundary. Also account for module size: `conversation.rs` is already 398 lines, so avoid adding more recovery logic there beyond perhaps a re-export if absolutely necessary.

files_changed.note: Added `recover_conversation`, a thin recovery-module helper delegating to `DurableConversation::recover`.
Added recovery tests for partial step replay, finished-message replay, expected_seq restoration, redelivery decisions, idempotent repeated recovery, and zero recovery writes.

dev.how: Conversation recovery intentionally delegates to DUR-005’s existing `DurableConversation::recover`, preserving the existing event-log replay implementation and expected-sequence calculation. Tests seed event logs and assert replayed `ConversationState`, `ResumeFrom(1)` for partially completed work, `Skip` for fully processed messages, and `expected_seq == last_event_sequence + 1`.

checklist.note: Durable conversations recover by replaying the event log through the DUR-005 recovery path and resume from the last completed step.

stories.note: Conversation state is automatically reconstructed on restart from the persisted event log.

acceptance.criterion: A conversation with events [MessageReceived, ProcessingStarted, StepCompleted(0)] recovers with step 0 complete and processing resumable from step 1.
acceptance.evidence: crates/liminal/src/durability/recovery.rs:64-68 delegates to DurableConversation::recover; test seeds MessageReceived/ProcessingStarted/StepCompleted(0) at crates/liminal/src/durability/recovery/tests.rs:52-67 and asserts last_completed_step("msg") == Some(0) plus ResumeFrom(1) at lines 89-95.

acceptance.criterion: A conversation with ProcessingFinished for a message recovers with that message marked as fully processed.
acceptance.evidence: Test seeds ProcessingFinished at crates/liminal/src/durability/recovery/tests.rs:69-83 and asserts is_fully_processed plus RedeliveryDecision::Skip at lines 97-101.

acceptance.criterion: Recovery delegates to DUR-005's replay logic, not a reimplementation.
acceptance.evidence: crates/liminal/src/durability/recovery.rs:64-68 is a thin call to DurableConversation::recover; DurableConversation::recover replays via replay_stream at crates/liminal/src/durability/conversation.rs:184-195 and replay_stream applies deserialized events at lines 357-391.

acceptance.criterion: The recovered conversation's expected_seq is set to last_event_sequence + 1.
acceptance.evidence: crates/liminal/src/durability/conversation.rs:372-390 tracks last_sequence and returns checked last + 1; recovery test asserts expected_seq() == 3 for three seeded events at crates/liminal/src/durability/recovery/tests.rs:89 and 97.

acceptance.criterion: No in-memory cache is consulted — only haematite.
acceptance.evidence: DurableConversation::recover reads from DurableStore::read_from in replay_stream at crates/liminal/src/durability/conversation.rs:357-367; recovery.rs only delegates at crates/liminal/src/durability/recovery.rs:64-68 and contains no cache path.

requirements.title: Recover consumer cursors from persisted offsets
requirements.spec: WHEN a consumer cursor recovers after crash, THE SYSTEM SHALL read the persisted offset from haematite using DUR-003's resume logic. The consumer SHALL resume processing from the stored offset, replaying any messages between the cursor and the partition head. IF no persisted offset exists, THE SYSTEM SHALL start from offset 0.
acceptance: A cursor with persisted offset 42 recovers and resumes from position 42.
A cursor with no persisted offset starts from position 0.
Recovery delegates to DUR-003's resume logic, not a reimplementation.
The recovered cursor is ready for checkpoint operations (cas will use the persisted offset as old_value).

files: docs/design/durability/briefs/DUR-003.json:14-67 — expected `ConsumerCursor` shape, `from_persisted`, checkpoint CAS key, and resume behavior.
docs/design/durability/briefs/DUR-003.json:88-120 — expected `replay_from(store, partition_key, offset)` pagination/deserialization behavior.
crates/liminal/src/durability/store.rs:27-39 — only available store reads are `read_from` and `scan`; cursor checkpoint must later use `cas`.
crates/liminal/src/durability/channel/storage.rs:152-156 — partition key format matches DUR-003: `"{channel_id}:{partition_index}"`.
crates/liminal/src/durability/error.rs:17-24,51-57 — CAS mismatch maps to `DurabilityError::CursorRegression`.
context: This checkout does not contain `crates/liminal/src/durability/cursor.rs` or `replay.rs`; DUR-006 should be implemented against the DUR-003 API once present, not by recreating cursor storage in recovery.

DUR-003’s intended cursor fields are `consumer_id`, `partition_key`, and `current_offset`, with `ConsumerCursor::from_persisted(...)` preserving the offset so the next checkpoint uses that value as CAS `old_value`.
The cursor CAS key convention is `"{consumer_id}:{partition_key}"`; recovery must use the same resume helper/key path as checkpoint or later checkpointing will regress/fail incorrectly.
Recovery should also return or trigger replay of messages from the recovered offset using DUR-003’s `replay_from`, not manual `MessageEnvelope` deserialization inside recovery.
scout.approach: Once DUR-003 APIs are available, make `recovery.rs` delegate to them: e.g. `recover_cursor(consumer_id, partition_key, store) -> Result<ConsumerCursor, DurabilityError>` should call the cursor resume/read helper (or `ConsumerCursor::resume(...)`) and default to offset `0` exactly as DUR-003 defines. If the recovery API is meant to include missed messages, add a wrapper that first recovers the cursor and then calls `replay_from(store.as_ref(), cursor.partition_key(), cursor.current_offset()).await`, returning both cursor and replayed envelopes in a `Debug` result struct. Keep the cursor’s `current_offset` equal to the persisted offset so subsequent `checkpoint` CAS uses it as `old_value`.
scout.notes: Because `cursor.rs` is absent, the implementer may need to branch from a stacked state that includes DUR-003 or coordinate dependency order. Do not compensate by implementing cursor resume from scratch in `recovery.rs`; that violates the brief boundary and creates duplicate key/serialization rules.

files_changed.note: Added `recover_cursor` and `recover_cursor_with_replay`, delegating cursor resume to `ConsumerCursor::resume` and missed-message replay to `replay_from`.
Added DUR-003-compatible `ConsumerCursor` with `from_persisted`, read-only `resume`, CAS-based `checkpoint`, and shared cursor key formatting.
Added DUR-003-compatible `replay_from` helper that pages partition logs with read_from and deserializes `MessageEnvelope`s.
Added `DurableStore::read_value` as the dedicated cursor-read operation for CAS-backed cursor offsets.
Added read-only `EventStore::read_value` for numeric values updated via CAS.
Declared and re-exported cursor and replay modules along with recovery helpers.
Updated existing fake store to implement the new `read_value` trait method.
Updated existing fake store to implement the new `read_value` trait method.
Updated integration-test fake store to implement the new `read_value` trait method.
Added cursor recovery tests for persisted offset 42, missing offset default 0, replay from recovered offset, idempotent repeated recovery, and checkpoint readiness via CAS old_value.

dev.how: The recovery API delegates cursor state loading to `ConsumerCursor::resume`, which reads the persisted CAS value using the shared cursor key and defaults to offset 0 when absent. `recover_cursor_with_replay` then delegates message replay to `replay_from`, keeping the recovered cursor at the persisted offset so the next checkpoint uses that value as the CAS old_value.
dev.deviation: DUR-003 cursor and replay modules were absent in this checkout. To avoid reimplementing cursor resume inside `recovery.rs`, I added the minimal DUR-003-compatible `cursor.rs` and `replay.rs` APIs plus a dedicated `DurableStore::read_value`/haematite read operation, then made DUR-006 recovery delegate to those helpers.

checklist.note: Cursor recovery reads the persisted offset, defaults to zero when absent, replays messages from that offset, and leaves checkpoint CAS ready to use the recovered old value.

stories.note: Consumer cursors can be automatically resumed on restart and replay messages between the cursor and partition head.

acceptance.criterion: A cursor with persisted offset 42 recovers and resumes from position 42.
acceptance.evidence: ConsumerCursor::resume reads the cursor value and constructs from_persisted at crates/liminal/src/durability/cursor.rs:39-52; test seeds offset 42 and asserts recovered.current_offset() == 42 at crates/liminal/src/durability/recovery/tests.rs:111-125.

acceptance.criterion: A cursor with no persisted offset starts from position 0.
acceptance.evidence: ConsumerCursor::resume defaults missing read_value to 0 at crates/liminal/src/durability/cursor.rs:46-47; test durability::recovery::tests::cursor_recovery_without_persisted_offset_starts_at_zero asserts offset 0 at crates/liminal/src/durability/recovery/tests.rs:165-180.

acceptance.criterion: Recovery delegates to DUR-003's resume logic, not a reimplementation.
acceptance.evidence: recover_cursor is a direct delegate to ConsumerCursor::resume at crates/liminal/src/durability/recovery.rs:76-82; recover_cursor_with_replay delegates replay to replay_from at crates/liminal/src/durability/recovery.rs:89-100.

acceptance.criterion: The recovered cursor is ready for checkpoint operations (cas will use the persisted offset as old_value).
acceptance.evidence: ConsumerCursor::checkpoint calls DurableStore::cas with self.current_offset as old_value at crates/liminal/src/durability/cursor.rs:97-99; the recovery test verifies no CAS during recovery, then checkpoint from recovered offset 42 to 45 succeeds and increments CAS count once at crates/liminal/src/durability/recovery/tests.rs:135-161.

issues: Found during hardening: ConsumerCursor::checkpoint originally allowed new_offset lower than current_offset when the stored CAS old_value matched, so a recovered cursor at 42 could have checkpointed 41 and regressed durable state. This violated the cursor-regression safety expected by CN6/DUR-003 and weakened the R3 checkpoint-readiness guarantee.
fixes: Added a pre-CAS regression guard in crates/liminal/src/durability/cursor.rs:90-95 returning DurabilityError::CursorRegression { stored: current_offset, attempted: new_offset } for lower offsets.

Added recovery test coverage at crates/liminal/src/durability/recovery/tests.rs:138-152 proving a checkpoint to 41 from recovered offset 42 fails, leaves the cursor/store at 42, and performs zero CAS calls before the valid checkpoint.

requirements.title: Guarantee idempotent replay across all recovery paths
requirements.spec: All recovery paths SHALL be idempotent: executing recovery twice on the same haematite state SHALL produce identical results. Channel sequence reconstruction SHALL be deterministic. Conversation state replay SHALL produce identical ConversationState. Cursor resume SHALL produce identical cursor position. Recovery SHALL NOT write to haematite — it is a read-only operation that reconstructs in-memory state.
acceptance: Running channel recovery twice produces the same partition sequences.
Running conversation recovery twice produces the same ConversationState (asserted via PartialEq or field comparison).
Running cursor recovery twice produces the same cursor offset.
Recovery performs zero DurableStore::append or DurableStore::cas calls — read-only.
No side effects: recovery does not modify haematite state.

files: crates/liminal/src/durability/store.rs:16-39 — recovery must only use `read_from` and delegated read-only helpers; no direct `append`/`cas`.
crates/liminal/src/durability/conversation.rs:82-120 — `ConversationState::replay/apply` use idempotent set/map updates.
crates/liminal/src/durability/conversation/tests.rs:118-146 — existing idempotency test pattern comparing repeated replay state.
crates/liminal/src/durability/channel/storage.rs:140-144 — recovered channel exposes all `next_sequences` for equality assertions.
crates/liminal/src/durability/conversation/tests.rs:219-304 — test `FakeStore` pattern tracks appends and reads; extend/copy into recovery tests to assert zero writes.
context: Recovery idempotency is mostly a property of using deterministic reads and existing replay/resume helpers; avoid mutable global state or cached recovery results.

For channel recovery, equality can be asserted on `next_sequences()` across two recovery runs. For conversation recovery, compare `recovered.state()` and `expected_seq()`. For cursor recovery, compare `current_offset` once DUR-003 exposes it.
Read-only verification should be enforced with tests using a fake store that increments `append_count` and `cas_count`; grep is useful but tests catch delegated accidental writes.
All public result structs introduced by recovery must derive/implement `Debug`; any new error variants are disallowed, so propagate `DurabilityError` from existing store/replay/resume calls.
scout.approach: Keep `recovery.rs` as a pure read/orchestration module: channel helper pages `read_from`; conversation helper delegates to `DurableConversation::recover`; cursor helper delegates to DUR-003 resume and replay. Add recovery-specific tests in the new module under `#[cfg(test)]` using a local `FakeStore` similar to `conversation/tests.rs`, with counters for reads/appends/cas. Run each recovery function twice on the same store state and assert equal outputs plus `append_count == 0` and `cas_count == 0`.
scout.notes: Be careful if using public constructors in tests to seed data: seeding via normal append APIs is fine before recovery, but capture write counts before invoking recovery and assert they do not change. Also keep `mod.rs` compliant with CN3: only `pub mod recovery;`/`pub use recovery::{...};`, no helper functions or type definitions there.

files_changed.note: Kept recovery helpers read-only and deterministic; recovery module contains no direct append or cas calls.
Added repeated channel/conversation/cursor recovery assertions and fake-store counters proving recovery performs zero append/cas operations.
Cursor resume is read-only and deterministic; checkpoint remains the only CAS write path and is outside recovery.
Replay helper is read-only and deterministic over the partition log.

dev.how: All recovery paths reconstruct state from store reads and delegated read-only helpers only: channel recovery compares repeated recovered sequences, conversation recovery compares repeated replayed state, and cursor recovery compares repeated offsets/replayed messages. Tests track append and CAS counters to confirm recovery has no side effects.

checklist.note: Repeated recovery over unchanged store state yields identical channel sequences, conversation state, and cursor position/replayed messages, with zero recovery writes.

acceptance.criterion: Running channel recovery twice produces the same partition sequences.
acceptance.evidence: test durability::recovery::tests::channel_recovery_reconstructs_partition_sequences compares the recovered channel sequences with a second recover_partition_sequences result at crates/liminal/src/durability/recovery/tests.rs:17-21.

acceptance.criterion: Running conversation recovery twice produces the same ConversationState (asserted via PartialEq or field comparison).
acceptance.evidence: test durability::recovery::tests::conversation_recovery_replays_partial_and_finished_state runs recover_conversation twice and asserts state equality at crates/liminal/src/durability/recovery/tests.rs:85-90.

acceptance.criterion: Running cursor recovery twice produces the same cursor offset.
acceptance.evidence: test durability::recovery::tests::cursor_recovery_resumes_offset_and_replays_missed_messages runs recover_cursor_with_replay twice and asserts both offsets are 42 at crates/liminal/src/durability/recovery/tests.rs:113-125; the empty-offset test also compares 0 at lines 169-178.

acceptance.criterion: Recovery performs zero DurableStore::append or DurableStore::cas calls — read-only.
acceptance.evidence: Recovery functions in crates/liminal/src/durability/recovery.rs:30-100 call read/delegate paths only; grep -RIn "append\|cas" crates/liminal/src/durability/recovery.rs returned no matches (exit 1). Tests assert append_count/cas_count stay zero for channel, conversation, and cursor recovery at crates/liminal/src/durability/recovery/tests.rs:22-23,102-103,135-136,179-180.

acceptance.criterion: No side effects: recovery does not modify haematite state.
acceptance.evidence: Read-only recovery is enforced by zero append/cas assertions across the recovery tests at crates/liminal/src/durability/recovery/tests.rs:22-23,102-103,135-136,179-180, and by the absence of append/cas calls in crates/liminal/src/durability/recovery.rs per grep verification.

issues: Same cursor regression issue found under R3 also affected R4's no-side-effects/idempotent recovery story after recovery handed back a checkpoint-ready cursor: a lower checkpoint could have modified the store backwards outside recovery.
fixes: The cursor checkpoint guard in crates/liminal/src/durability/cursor.rs:90-95 prevents post-recovery backward side effects; the new assertion at crates/liminal/src/durability/recovery/tests.rs:138-152 verifies the store remains at offset 42 and cas_count remains 0 after a rejected lower checkpoint.

boundaries: SHALL NOT reimplement replay or resume logic — delegate to DUR-003 and DUR-005.
SHALL NOT write to haematite during recovery — recovery is read-only.
SHALL NOT implement dedup cache recovery — dedup entries are reconstructed from haematite on demand.
SHALL NOT add recovery-specific error types — use DurabilityError from DUR-001.
SHALL NOT implement cross-node recovery or replication — this is single-node recovery only.
verification: cargo check compiles the recovery module with no errors.
Test: channel recovery from populated haematite produces correct sequence numbers.
Test: conversation recovery produces identical state on two runs (idempotency).
Test: cursor recovery with and without persisted offset.
grep -rn 'append\|cas' recovery.rs returns no write calls (read-only verification).
